justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Four bounded contexts clearly identified — Route Planning, Delivery Management, Fleet Management, Customer Notification. (2) Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out in escalations and edge cases, particularly route recalculation depending on delivery lifecycle state changes and vehicle class as a Fleet→Route constraint. (3) Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (BR-2), stop time correctly marked invariant:false (BR-8/BR-9), notification window invariant:false (BR-6). (4) Ten acceptance criteria provided, well above the 4 minimum. (5) Route recalculation triggers correctly enumerated: new delivery added (BR-3), delivery failed (BR-4), driver deviation (BR-5).

observations.text: Four well-defined bounded contexts with clear ownership boundaries and explicit statement that contexts must communicate via integration events, not shared state
10 business rules with thoughtful invariant classifications and strong justifications — particularly good reasoning on why recalculation triggers are invariant:false (policy, not data integrity)
10 acceptance criteria covering happy path, edge cases, and cross-context scenarios like AC-7 (vehicle class filtering) and AC-10 (zone isolation)
Excellent edge case analysis identifying multi-driver zones, recalculation cascades, and notification lifecycle gaps — these are genuine production risks
Escalations are well-structured with options and recommendations, surfacing real architectural decisions for MJ

BR-3/BR-4/BR-5 (recalculation triggers) marked invariant:false, but the rubric expected invariant:true — the agent's reasoning is defensible (recalculation policy is tunable) but diverges from expected classification

Cross-context dependency between route recalculation and delivery lifecycle explicitly identified in bounded_context description and in escalations, meeting the integration point surfacing requirement

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automatically plans and dynamically adjusts delivery routes for drivers during their shifts. It spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, delivery lifecycle, fleet/driver management, and customer notifications. The feature encodes real-time operational logistics where route state is mutable and reactive to field events.","bounded_context":"This feature touches at minimum four bounded contexts: (1)
